codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested settings section, navigation entry, beacon panel, daemon command bridge, and background push/refresh loop, but the reviewed implementation does not fully satisfy the contract because its periodic beacon payloads read commit metadata from a project-status cache that never includes those fields for most repositories. As a result, remote machines can still show empty last-commit details even though the panel UI expects them.

issues.description: `beaconRepos` builds each repo payload from `projectsState.statuses[project.path]`, expecting `lastCommitHash`, `lastCommitMessage`, and `lastCommitTimestamp`. But `ProjectsContext`'s `ProjectStatus` type and its `get_all_project_statuses` refresh path only track `currentBranch`, cleanliness, and ahead/behind counts, so the background timer usually pushes blank commit metadata for non-selected projects. That breaks the feature requirement that the beacon panel show each remote repo's last commit, and it is exactly why the later fix feature `fix-beacon-machine-name-and-commit-metadata` had to patch this area.

The shared `MessageType` union was not extended with `sync_beacon_check_gh`, `sync_beacon_validate_gist`, `sync_beacon_pull`, or `sync_beacon_push`. Runtime still works because `send()` accepts arbitrary strings, but the shared frontend/backend command contract is now stale and no longer documents the new Sync Beacon message surface.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `tauri-fullstack` skill still treats browser verification as a full manual validation path, but Sync Beacon interactions depend on Tauri runtime APIs and gh auth, so browser-only checks can only verify route wiring and basic layout. The worker followed that reality and explicitly downgraded verification to a smoke check.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The handoff and transcript for worker session `e6095314-e9f2-4cdc-8ad6-0223cb73482d` note that browser validation could only confirm `/app` rendering because Sync Beacon commands require Tauri, while `.factory/skills/tauri-fullstack/SKILL.md` section 6 still instructs workers to use the frontend dev server + browser verification as the manual verification step. `.factory/library/user-testing.md` already documents that Sync beacon Gist operations require Tauri invoke.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture docs still do not record that `ProjectsContext` status polling omits last-commit metadata, even though Sync Beacon tried to depend on that cache for its background payload generation. That gap made it easy for the frontend feature to assume commit fields existed for every repo when they did not.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `packages/ui/src/lib/projects-context.tsx` defines `ProjectStatus` with only `currentBranch`, `uncommittedCount`, `ahead`, `behind`, and `isClean`, while `packages/ui/src/lib/sync-beacon-context.tsx` reads `lastCommitHash`, `lastCommitMessage`, and `lastCommitTimestamp` from that cache. `.factory/library/architecture.md` documents command flow and settings flow, but not the limitations of the project-status cache that matter for beacon payload generation.

summary: Fail. The reviewed Sync Beacon frontend commit wires up the requested UI and timer flow, but its background payload generation relies on project status data that does not actually include repo commit metadata, so the panel cannot reliably satisfy the required last-commit display until the later follow-up fix. I also noted a non-blocking shared-contract gap in `MessageType` and two shared-state observations about skill guidance and undocumented project-status cache limitations.
